Strictly Confidential – MNPI – Project Banyan – Embargoed until 30 June 2026, 08:00 SGT.
Aurora Straits Holdings Ltd. (SGX-ST Mainboard) incident report: vendor SFTP credential compromise detected on 28 May 2026 at 21:40 SGT by SOC alert, contained within 45 minutes.
Unauthorised access exposed PDPA-covered data (names, emails, and mobile numbers of 37 job applicants) and one internal Board memo attachment; no NRIC copies were stored in the affected directory.
MNPI exposure risk: draft Share Purchase Agreement and term sheet for Project Banyan (proposed acquisition of 75% of Meridian Docks Pte. Ltd. for S$240,000,000) were present in the same vendor exchange.
SPA clause 8.2 (negated MAC): any cyber incident disclosed prior to Closing shall not, by itself, constitute a Material Adverse Effect for purposes of Conditions Precedent.
Status: forensic timeline indicates data exfiltration window < 2 hours; no customer or payroll records accessed; preliminary impact to FY2026 revenue estimated at ≤1.5%.
Regulatory/listing considerations: under the SFA and SGX Listing Manual Rule 703, the information is likely price-sensitive; recommend trading halt and embargo on external communications until 30 June 2026 08:00 SGT pending final forensic report.
PDPA actions: notify PDPC within 3 business days under Part VIA; implement credential rotation and vendor hardening; prepare individual notifications for affected applicants via dpo@aurorastraits.sg.
IR draft (not for release) prepared for potential post-embargo announcement; queries to Ms. Rina Koh, Head of Investor Relations, rina.koh@aurorastraits.sg; restrict circulation to Board and deal team only.
Deal checklist: confirm no leak paths from advisors; rotate vendor keys; verify that only generally available information is used in any analyst engagement; cross-check public sources (e.g., 2024 supplier list on our website).
Board memo note: if SPA executed before disclosure, include carve-out and covenant to cooperate on cyber remediation; if not, amend term sheet to add escrow of S$12,000,000 (5%) for post-closing claims.
